# Cost Forecast — 2026-06-29

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-06-22 → 2026-06-28 (7 days; ex-Cosmos, ex-Postgres — non-Postgres run-rate from survivor services only)
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

> **Status check on Postgres billing.** This is the second post-migration report. The Postgres server (`psql-town-crier-shared`, `Standard_B1ms`, provisioned 2026-06-26) has been `Ready` and serving prod for 3 full days now. The Jun 28 actuals still show **£0.00** for `Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL` — first-billing latency continues. Rate-card estimate (£0.445/day) is unchanged from the 2026-06-28 report. The Postgres billing lag is the single biggest open variable in this forecast; it will resolve by 30 Jun–1 Jul. The rest of the picture is confirmed and stable.

## Headline

| Horizon          | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta    | Confidence |
|------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|---------:|------------|
| Rest of month (29–30 Jun) | £2.07    | £1.84          | +£0.23   | Medium     |
| Next 30 days     | £31.02             | £43.39         | −£12.37  | Medium     |
| Next quarter     | £94.09             | ~£129          | ~−£35    | Low        |

**TL;DR:** The post-migration steady-state is confirmed at **~£1.03/day (≈ £31.5/mo)**, identical to yesterday's reading. Azure's near-term forecast (rest of month, £1.84) is low by ~£0.23 because Postgres hasn't yet appeared in actuals — the two effects cancel partially. On the 30-day and quarterly horizons Azure is materially overstated by £12–35 because it is still extrapolating the dead Cosmos line (~£1/day ghost) and a phantom July spike artifact. No new cost-reduction opportunities were found: the subscription is running lean at every measurable lever.

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **Cosmos (`cosmos-town-crier-shared`) — destroyed 2026-06-27, three clean £0 days, still inflating Azure's 30-day projection.** Cosmos cost £28.53 in June (Jun 1–27), peaking at £1.56/day (25 Jun) before the cutover dropped it to £0.61 (26 Jun) → £0.00008 (27 Jun, final billing flash) → £0.00 (28 Jun). No `Microsoft.DocumentDB` resource remains in the subscription; confirmed `az cosmosdb list` returns `[]`. Azure's linear model carries a ~£0.26/day Cosmos ghost forward, accounting for roughly **£7.7 of its £12.4 over-statement** on the next-30-days horizon. Commits: `21e8fda3` (2026-06-27, remove per-env DBs/containers) and `26b1604a` (2026-06-27, destroy shared Cosmos account).
- **The £7.43/day spike, 12–21 Jul (Azure forecast artifact).** Azure's model once again produces a 10-day plateau at £7.43/day starting 12 Jul (same artifact noted in the 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-28 reports). No current resource can produce it. Stripping this artifact eliminates essentially all of the remaining over-statement beyond the Cosmos ghost. The artifact appears to be Azure extrapolating the rapid Cosmos-growth ramp from mid-June, producing a confidence-band spike it cannot justify from the resource graph.
- **Postgres (`psql-town-crier-shared`) — not yet in actuals, understating near-term.** Three consecutive days of billing zeros for a live B1ms server are first-billing latency, not real zeros. Azure's near-term forecast accordingly omits this resource, making its rest-of-month figure (£1.84) about **£0.90 too low** versus bottom-up. This effect is swamped on the 30-day horizon by the Cosmos/spike over-statement.

The two differ by ~£0.13/day — within Postgres rate-card uncertainty. Once Postgres actuals land the figure will self-calibrate.*

## Recommendations

No actionable cost-saving opportunities were found above the £2/month threshold at current scale.

**What was checked and cleared:**

- **Cosmos → Postgres migration saving: ~£18/month realised.** Cosmos peaked at £1.56/day (25 Jun); Postgres replaces it at £0.445/day. Net saving ~£0.83–1.11/day (£25–33/month reduction in DB costs). Already done — no bead needed.
- **Container Registry (Basic, 82 MB / 10 GB):** 99% free quota headroom. No purge policy needed.
- **Log Analytics table plans:** `AppTraces` and `ContainerAppConsoleLogs` are already on Basic. `AppRequests`, `AppDependencies`, and others are on Analytics — but total Azure Monitor spend is £0.003/day (< £0.10/month) because overall ingestion is well under the 5 GB/month mark where Analytics billing kicks in. Switching remaining tables to Basic at this ingestion rate saves £0. Revisit if monthly ingestion ever approaches 5 GB.
- **Service Bus tier:** Already Basic. Zero topics. No upgrade needed.
- **Container App min replicas:** Prod ACA is at `minReplicas: 1` (intentional cold-start fix, tc-9u1r/tc-e972); dev ACA is at `minReplicas: 0` (correct). No change.
- **ACA revisions:** Prod has 1 active revision; dev has 1 active revision at 0 replicas. No revision sprawl.
- **Orphaned resources:** `id-town-crier-cosmos-data` (managed identity) remains in the `shared` Pulumi stack and in Azure even though Cosmos is destroyed. Managed identities are free in Azure — no cost impact. This is a code-cleanup item, not a cost item.

**Next check:**
Re-run `/cost-forecast` once Postgres actuals land (expected 30 Jun–1 Jul) to confirm the rate-card estimate. No other follow-up needed.

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals window:** 2026-04-30 → 2026-06-28 (60 days), daily granularity, grouped by `ServiceName` and by daily totals, via Cost Management REST API (`api-version=2023-11-01`).
- **Azure forecast:** Cost Management forecast API, Jun 29–Jul 28, daily granularity. Quarterly figure estimated by extending the 30-day daily distribution.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** All three stacks (`shared`, `dev`, `prod`) exported and cross-referenced against `az resource list`. 35 Azure resources, all accounted for. One drift item: `id-town-crier-cosmos-data` still in shared stack despite Cosmos deletion — zero cost, code cleanup only.
- **Exclusions:** One-off charges (Jun 14 deployment spike: £2.20/day vs £1.30 norm, attributable to dual .NET+Go ACA churn during cut-over week). Excluded from run-rate baseline. Pre-Jun-10 period (no ACA) not used in forward baseline.
- **Postgres rate card:** `Standard_B1ms` UK South — compute £0.346/day (£0.01442/hr × 24h), storage £0.099/day (32 GB × £0.0946/GB/month ÷ 30). Total £0.445/day. No actuals yet; confirmed resource is `Ready` and serving prod.
- **Known gap:** Postgres billing latency is still unresolved as of 2026-06-29 morning. The first real actuals may differ from the rate card by ±10–15%.
